title: Calycothrix sapphirina
intro: Calycothrix sapphirina, also known as Sapphire Calycothrix, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to New Caledonia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising treatment for various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related conditions. Calycothrix sapphirina contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems are used to make extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.

Traditional healers in South America have reportedly used Calycothrix sapphirina to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
